sections.paragraph:
οἰνόμελι, ιτος, τό (οἶνος, μέλι; Carneades [II b.c.] in Diog. L. 4, 64; Polyb. 12, 2, 7; Diod S 5, 34, 2; Plut., Mor. 196e; 733e; Diosc., Mat. Med. 5, 8 W; Sext. Emp., Adv. Math. 6, 44, 9. Loanw. in rabb.) wine mixed with honey, a drink someth. like mead. In our lit. only fig., of dissidents: θανάσιμον φάρμακον διδόναι μετὰ οἰνομέλιτος give a deadly poison mixed with honeyed wine (Miambi 7, 45 in Herodas p. 50 C. [=Select Papyri, ed. DPage ’41, III p. 356, 42] φάρμακον θανάσιμον μετʼ οἰνομέλιτος) ITr 6:2.

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

someth. someth. = something

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 700
